title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Mukaijima (also referred to as Mochizuki) stops the group to explain the killer's motive and the pattern behind the recent murders. He reveals that the various "unnecessary" props and circumstances of each crime were intentional challenges meant to demonstrate the killer's superiority. By listing the specific elements of each murder—the iron boots on Itô, the locked room and kumquat seeds with Umay, the white face of Akagi, the nakedness of Shizuki, the coffin of Hozumi, the twins Kanae and Michiyo, the LP record of Yasuko, and the hat—he identifies a theme. The killer has been performing perfect imitations of the "nationality series" by Ellery Queen, specifically: *The Dutch Shoe Mystery*, *The Chinese Orange Mystery*, *The French Powder Mystery*, *The Spanish Shawl Mystery*, *The Greek Coffin Mystery*, *The Siamese Twin Mystery*, *The American Gun Mystery*, and *The Roman Hat Mystery*.

Upon entering the chapel, the group discovers a final tableau: Kaken’s body is bound to a Russian cross, with his head placed on the altar. Mukaijima declares that "The Mystery of the Egyptian Cross" is complete. Mutsu then begins to explain the failure to identify Mizuki as the murderer sooner. He notes that while Itô’s severed feet were hidden in a desk drawer under an unopened envelope addressed to the Higashiyama Detective Agency, the killer showed no interest in the letter. The police chief observes that the killer did not open the envelope, suggesting the killer had no motive to uncover the contents of the commission Itô was preparing.
